visa_types.name: Private Visitor
visa_types.documents_required:
Please gather the relevant documents provided in the document checklist based on the purpose of your trip (business, tourism, family visit etc.) 
Please note that your passport should be valid for a minimum of three months after expiry of the visa; must be issued within the last ten years and must have two blank pages - if you do not have two blank pages to which a visa
                            can be affixed, a new passport must be obtained before submitting your application.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Tourist]

[Table:   Tourist]
Please Note:
- Applicants applying from Edmonton, Toronto and Vancouver need to pay mandatory courier charges of Euro 6 (CAD 9.5) towards one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Ottawa back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across Canada
- The fee is non-refundable
- All VFS Fees can be paid by Debit/Credit Card only.
- AMEX is not accepted.
visa_types.processing_time:
STANDARD PROCESSING TIMES 
Due to unusually high volumes of visa applications the processing time is currently longer than usual, and there are fewer available appointment slots at VFS Global.
The processing time for visa applications are currently up to 15 days. We regret the inconvenience this may cause, but assure that applications are handled as swiftly as possible.
A visa application can be launched as early as 6 months prior to the arrival date in Schengen, and we strongly encourage applicants to apply as early as possible. It is possible to apply up to 6 months prior to the desired date of entry Here is why:
- It can take up to 14 days to obtain an appointment with VFS for submitting your application.
- Case processing does not begin until the application arrives at the Consulate General of Denmark in New York. Hence, your application is not launched when you apply online, or when you have your appointment with VFS. The shipping
                                of your documents to and from the Royal Danish Embassy in Ottawa adds 1-2 days to the processing time.
- An application lodged less than 15 calendar days before the intended departure may be accepted at VFS, but the final decision might be taken after the date of departure.
You can find the information on which third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation on the Visa Code Handbook, Annex 16 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%c3%a5ndbogen).
Please note that case processing may be extended if further scrutiny is required
Also missing documentation at the time of the appointment at VFS may delay the processing time.
Please also note that the Consulate General cannot be held responsible for technical difficulties, human errors or shipping delays in the processing of visa applications.
It is the applicant's responsibility to notify the Danish Consulate General immediately, if incorrect information on the visa sticker has been
                            detected.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
You should apply for a private visitor visa, if you are travelling to Denmark for private visit.
A visa allows you to stay a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period in Denmark and is normally valid for the entire Schengen region.
Decisions about visa applications will normally be 15 days from the day the Danish Consulate General in New York receives the application, excluding submission and administration time at VFS. In some cases applications will require further enquiry or documentation implying that the processing time might exceed the 15 days.
Applicants might also be asked to appear for an Interview via the Danish Embassy in Ottawa.
A visa does not allow you to work in Denmark unless the Immigration Service has explicitly granted you this right.
For more information about how to apply for a visa, please click here (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
You should apply for a private visitor visa, if you are travelling to Denmark for private visit.
A visa allows you to stay a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period in Denmark and is normally valid for the entire Schengen region.
Decisions about visa applications will normally be 15 days from the day the Danish Consulate General in New York receives the application, excluding submission and administration time at VFS. In some cases applications will require further enquiry or documentation implying that the processing time might exceed the 15 days.
Applicants might also be asked to appear for an Interview via the Danish Embassy in Ottawa.
A visa does not allow you to work in Denmark unless the Immigration Service has explicitly granted you this right.
For more information about how to apply for a visa, please click here (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/)

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Tourist]

[Table:   Tourist]
Please Note:
- Applicants applying from Edmonton, Toronto and Vancouver need to pay mandatory courier charges of Euro 6 (CAD 9.5) towards one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Ottawa back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across Canada
- The fee is non-refundable
- All VFS Fees can be paid by Debit/Credit Card only.
- AMEX is not accepted.

## Documents Required
Please gather the relevant documents provided in the document checklist based on the purpose of your trip (business, tourism, family visit etc.) 
Please note that your passport should be valid for a minimum of three months after expiry of the visa; must be issued within the last ten years and must have two blank pages - if you do not have two blank pages to which a visa
                            can be affixed, a new passport must be obtained before submitting your application.

## Photo Specifications
The photographs must meet the following ICAO requirements: 
- Colour photographs
- Light background
- Taken up close
- Well lit
- Taken straight on
- Up-to-date
- Without headgear
- Without sunglasses
- With mouth closed
- Photograph should measure 35 mm x 45 mm (1 3/8 x 1 ¾ inch)
- Height of head should measure 70 - 80 percent of height of photograph or 32 mm – 36 mm (1 2/8 to 1 ½ inch)

## Processing Time
STANDARD PROCESSING TIMES 
Due to unusually high volumes of visa applications the processing time is currently longer than usual, and there are fewer available appointment slots at VFS Global.
The processing time for visa applications are currently up to 15 days. We regret the inconvenience this may cause, but assure that applications are handled as swiftly as possible.
A visa application can be launched as early as 6 months prior to the arrival date in Schengen, and we strongly encourage applicants to apply as early as possible. It is possible to apply up to 6 months prior to the desired date of entry Here is why:
- It can take up to 14 days to obtain an appointment with VFS for submitting your application.
- Case processing does not begin until the application arrives at the Consulate General of Denmark in New York. Hence, your application is not launched when you apply online, or when you have your appointment with VFS. The shipping
                                of your documents to and from the Royal Danish Embassy in Ottawa adds 1-2 days to the processing time.
- An application lodged less than 15 calendar days before the intended departure may be accepted at VFS, but the final decision might be taken after the date of departure.
You can find the information on which third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation on the Visa Code Handbook, Annex 16 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%c3%a5ndbogen).
Please note that case processing may be extended if further scrutiny is required
Also missing documentation at the time of the appointment at VFS may delay the processing time.
Please also note that the Consulate General cannot be held responsible for technical difficulties, human errors or shipping delays in the processing of visa applications.
It is the applicant's responsibility to notify the Danish Consulate General immediately, if incorrect information on the visa sticker has been
                            detected.
                        

## Online Application Form
- Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa Portal (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form.
Note:
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS by clicking here. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted, and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. For appointment related question, kindly reach out to our support desk (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/can/en/dnk/contact-us) or visa application centre.
Refer to sample application form - CLICK HERE

visa_types.name: Short Term Stay
visa_types.overview:
Short-stay Schengen visa (90 days or less)
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
See the list of countries whose citizens need a visa to enter Denmark (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/need_visa/who_needs_visa.htm).
A visa grants you the right to spend a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period in the Schengen region.
visa_types.full_text:
## Short-stay Schengen visa (90 days or less)
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
See the list of countries whose citizens need a visa to enter Denmark (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/visa/need_visa/who_needs_visa.htm).
A visa grants you the right to spend a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period in the Schengen region.

visa_types.name: Family Reunification
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Family Reunification]
Please Note:
- • Applicants applying from Edmonton, Toronto and Vancouver need to pay mandatory courier charges of Euro 6 (CAD 9.5) towards one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Ottawa back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across Canada
- • The fee is non-refundable
- • All VFS Fees can be paid by Debit/Credit Card only.

AMEX is not accepted
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Residence and/or work permit must be obtained before entering Denmark.
If you have close relatives in Denmark, you can apply for a residence permit on the grounds of family reunification.
Provided that certain conditions are met, family reunification can be granted to:
Spouses, registered partners and cohabiting partners (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification)
Children under the age of 15 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification)
If you are granted a residence permit, it will be a temporary one. You can apply for an extension of your residence permit, and after a certain period of time, you can apply for a permanent residence permit. You must still meet the conditions for your original residence permit.
Normally, a residence permit as a family reunified person gives you the right to work in Denmark.
Please note that special rules apply to EU/EEA citizens and Swiss citizens residing in Denmark under the EU regulations on free movement. Read more under EU and Nordic Citizens (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Residence-as-a-Nordic-citizen-or-EU-or-EEA-citizen).
Also note that special rules apply if the person residing in Denmark (the spouse/partner or the parent) is a student (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Study).
Furthermore, special rules apply to Danish citizens who are applying for family reunification under the EU regulations on free movement. Read more about family reunification for Danish citizens under EU law. (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Residence-as-a-Nordic-citizen-or-EU-or-EEA-citizen/EU-Family-member-Danish-citizen)
Where to apply:
All applications will be handled by the Danish Embassy in Ottawa. Applicants can submit and have their biometrics recorded at these locations (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/can/en/dnk/contact-us).
What to submit:
Checklist for family reunification for spouses
Application packet FA1 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification/Spouse-or-cohabiting-partner)
Checklist for family reunification for children
Application packet FA6 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification/Child?anchor=canyouapply)
Application packet FA7 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification/Child?anchor=canyouapply)
Checklist for family reunification with other family members
Application packet SG1 (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification/Other-family-member?anchor=canyouapply)
Standard processing times for residence permit applications, including family reunification:
Please note: Your original passport must be submitted to VFS Global and will be sent to the Royal Danish Embassy in Ottawa for verification purposes. The passport will be returned to you usually within a week. For more information please visit: https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit)
Case processing times for The Danish Immigration Service can be found here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/News-Front-Page/2017/11/US_indfoerer_nye_maal_for_sagsbehandlingstid)
The Consulate General advises against making any final travel arrangements, particularly purchase of non-refundable air tickets for a specific date, prior to approval of your application.
To download the forms your computer must have Acrobat Reader installed. Acrobat Reader can be downloaded here (https://www.adobe.com/ca/).
For further information please visit the web-site of the Danish Immigration Service http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/ (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Residence and/or work permit must be obtained before entering Denmark.
If you have close relatives in Denmark, you can apply for a residence permit on the grounds of family reunification.
Provided that certain conditions are met, family reunification can be granted to:
Spouses, registered partners and cohabiting partners (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification)
Children under the age of 15 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification)
If you are granted a residence permit, it will be a temporary one. You can apply for an extension of your residence permit, and after a certain period of time, you can apply for a permanent residence permit. You must still meet the conditions for your original residence permit.
Normally, a residence permit as a family reunified person gives you the right to work in Denmark.
Please note that special rules apply to EU/EEA citizens and Swiss citizens residing in Denmark under the EU regulations on free movement. Read more under EU and Nordic Citizens (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Residence-as-a-Nordic-citizen-or-EU-or-EEA-citizen).
Also note that special rules apply if the person residing in Denmark (the spouse/partner or the parent) is a student (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Study).
Furthermore, special rules apply to Danish citizens who are applying for family reunification under the EU regulations on free movement. Read more about family reunification for Danish citizens under EU law. (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Residence-as-a-Nordic-citizen-or-EU-or-EEA-citizen/EU-Family-member-Danish-citizen)
Where to apply:
All applications will be handled by the Danish Embassy in Ottawa. Applicants can submit and have their biometrics recorded at these locations (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/can/en/dnk/contact-us).
What to submit:
Checklist for family reunification for spouses
Application packet FA1 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification/Spouse-or-cohabiting-partner)
Checklist for family reunification for children
Application packet FA6 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification/Child?anchor=canyouapply)
Application packet FA7 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification/Child?anchor=canyouapply)
Checklist for family reunification with other family members
Application packet SG1 (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Family/Family-reunification/Other-family-member?anchor=canyouapply)
Standard processing times for residence permit applications, including family reunification:
Please note: Your original passport must be submitted to VFS Global and will be sent to the Royal Danish Embassy in Ottawa for verification purposes. The passport will be returned to you usually within a week. For more information please visit: https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit)
Case processing times for The Danish Immigration Service can be found here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/News-Front-Page/2017/11/US_indfoerer_nye_maal_for_sagsbehandlingstid)
The Consulate General advises against making any final travel arrangements, particularly purchase of non-refundable air tickets for a specific date, prior to approval of your application.
To download the forms your computer must have Acrobat Reader installed. Acrobat Reader can be downloaded here (https://www.adobe.com/ca/).
For further information please visit the web-site of the Danish Immigration Service http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/ (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/).

## Visa Fees

[Family Reunification]
Please Note:
- • Applicants applying from Edmonton, Toronto and Vancouver need to pay mandatory courier charges of Euro 6 (CAD 9.5) towards one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Ottawa back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across Canada
- • The fee is non-refundable
- • All VFS Fees can be paid by Debit/Credit Card only.

AMEX is not accepted

visa_types.visa_fees:
[Family Reunification]
Please Note:

- • Applicants applying from Edmonton, Toronto and Vancouver need to pay mandatory courier charges of Euro 6 (CAD 9.5) towards one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Ottawa back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across Canada.
- • The fee is non-refundable

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Several Canadian educational institutions have exchange programs with several Danish educational institutions. If you are planning to study for more than 3 months in Denmark you must apply for a student permit.
Where to apply:
All applications will be handled by the Danish Embassy in Ottawa.
What to submit:
Checklist for submission of student permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit)
Checklist for online submission of student permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/practical-information/residence-permit/online-applications/)
Please note: Your original passport must be submitted to VFS Global and will be sent to the Royal Danish Embassy in Ottawa for verification purposes. The passport will be returned to you usually within a week. For more information please visit: https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit)
The Consulate General advises against making any final travel arrangements, particularly purchase of non-refundable air tickets for a specific date, prior to approval of your application.
Standard processing times for residence permit applications:
Case processing times for The Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) can be found here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/SIRI/Case-processing-times-in-SIRI/?anchor=71F61776C825468FB501A8B522130C03&callbackItem=1B4A751D509747158D4679B344E5F83D&callbackAnchor=60AE0A091828425B9E674E276E6FB986).
Since online submission of applications require biometrics to be obtained within 2 weeks from the online submission it is recommended to obtain an appointment with VFS Global prior to the online submission of the application since it can take up to 14 days to obtain an appointment with VFS Global.

visa_types.name: Red Carpet
visa_types.overview:
The Red Carpet Program is a simplified procedures for obtaining a Danish Schengen visas for applicants who travel regularly to Denmark for business purposes. The aim is to provide those companies, organizations, NGO’s etc. that have business relations with a Danish company or organization A fast track visa service.
The Red Carpet Program is open to private and public companies or organizations provided they meet the requirements mentioned on the local Danish embassy’s website. The accreditation of a company or an organization must have taken place before an employee/associate (the visa applicant) can benefit from the Red Carpet Program.
Companies, organizations, NGOs etc. that are interested in obtaining a Red Carpet agreement should contact the Danish Consulate General in New York.
For more information about Red Carpet Program, please click here (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/company-accredition/)
visa_types.full_text:
The Red Carpet Program is a simplified procedures for obtaining a Danish Schengen visas for applicants who travel regularly to Denmark for business purposes. The aim is to provide those companies, organizations, NGO’s etc. that have business relations with a Danish company or organization A fast track visa service.
The Red Carpet Program is open to private and public companies or organizations provided they meet the requirements mentioned on the local Danish embassy’s website. The accreditation of a company or an organization must have taken place before an employee/associate (the visa applicant) can benefit from the Red Carpet Program.
Companies, organizations, NGOs etc. that are interested in obtaining a Red Carpet agreement should contact the Danish Consulate General in New York.
For more information about Red Carpet Program, please click here (https://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/company-accredition/)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Denmark offers a wide variety of work and residence permits to foreign nationals who have an interest in exploring life in Denmark, the Faroe Islands or Greenland. Visa-exempt nationals, including Canadian citizens, can visit for up to 90 days without a visa. If you plan to work, study, volunteer or stay for more than 90 days, a residence or work permit is usually required.
Where to apply:
All applications will be handled by the Danish Embassy in Ottawa. Applicants can submit and have their biometrics recorded at these locations (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/can/en/dnk/attend-centre).
What to submit:
The application form (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/SIRI/Application-forms---SIRI) contains a detailed description of how you and the employer should complete the form, and which documents you must attach.
AR1 online (https://blanket.nyidanmark.dk/xform/formularer/ar1.form.aspx), in which you and your employer each are required to complete separate parts of the form
AR6 online (https://blanket.nyidanmark.dk/xform/formularer/ar67.form.aspx), in which you grant your employer power of attorney to apply on your behalf
Please note: Your original passport must be submitted to VFS Global and will be sent to the Royal Danish Embassy in Ottawa for verification purposes. The passport will be returned to you usually within a week. For more information please visit: https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit)
Checklist for submission of residence- and work permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/practical-information/residence-permit/residence-and-or-work-permits/)
Checklists where only biometrics recording is required:
Checklist for online submission of the residence- and work permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/practical-information/residence-permit/online-applications/)
Information about residence and work schemes in Denmark can be found here. (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB)
Standard processing times for residence permit applications:
Case processing times for The Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) can be found here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/SIRI/Case-processing-times-in-SIRI/?anchor=71F61776C825468FB501A8B522130C03&callbackItem=1B4A751D509747158D4679B344E5F83D&callbackAnchor=60AE0A091828425B9E674E276E6FB986).
Since online submission of applications require biometrics to be obtained within 2 weeks from the online submission it is recommended to obtain an appointment with VFS Global prior to the online submission of the application since it can take up to 14 days to obtain an appointment with VFS Global.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Denmark offers a wide variety of work and residence permits to foreign nationals who have an interest in exploring life in Denmark, the Faroe Islands or Greenland. Visa-exempt nationals, including Canadian citizens, can visit for up to 90 days without a visa. If you plan to work, study, volunteer or stay for more than 90 days, a residence or work permit is usually required.
Where to apply:
All applications will be handled by the Danish Embassy in Ottawa. Applicants can submit and have their biometrics recorded at these locations (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/can/en/dnk/attend-centre).

AR1 online (https://blanket.nyidanmark.dk/xform/formularer/ar1.form.aspx), in which you and your employer each are required to complete separate parts of the form
AR6 online (https://blanket.nyidanmark.dk/xform/formularer/ar67.form.aspx), in which you grant your employer power of attorney to apply on your behalf

The passport will be returned to you usually within a week. For more information please visit: https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit)
Checklist for submission of residence- and work permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/practical-information/residence-permit/residence-and-or-work-permits/)
Checklists where only biometrics recording is required:

Information about residence and work schemes in Denmark can be found here. (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB)
Standard processing times for residence permit applications:
Case processing times for The Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) can be found here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/SIRI/Case-processing-times-in-SIRI/?anchor=71F61776C825468FB501A8B522130C03&callbackItem=1B4A751D509747158D4679B344E5F83D&callbackAnchor=60AE0A091828425B9E674E276E6FB986).
Since online submission of applications require biometrics to be obtained within 2 weeks from the online submission it is recommended to obtain an appointment with VFS Global prior to the online submission of the application since it can take up to 14 days to obtain an appointment with VFS Global.

## Visa Fees

[Family Reunification]
Please Note:
- • Applicants applying from Edmonton, Toronto and Vancouver need to pay mandatory courier charges of Euro 6 (CAD 9.5) towards one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Ottawa back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across Canada
- • The fee is non-refundable
- • All VFS Fees can be paid by Debit/Credit Card only.

AMEX is not accepted

visa_types.processing_time:
STANDARD PROCESSING TIMES 
Due to unusually high volumes of visa applications the processing time is currently longer than usual, and there are fewer available appointment slots at VFS Global.
The processing time for visa applications are currently up to 15 days. We regret the inconvenience this may cause, but assure that applications are handled as swiftly as possible.
A visa application can be launched as early as 6 months prior to the arrival date in Schengen, and we strongly encourage applicants to apply as early as possible. It is possible to apply up to 6 months prior to the desired date of entry Here is why:
- It can take up to 14 days to obtain an appointment with VFS for submitting your application.
- Case processing does not begin until the application arrives at the Consulate General of Denmark in New York. Hence, your application is not launched when you apply online, or when you have your appointment with VFS. The shipping
                                of your documents to and from the Royal Danish Embassy in Ottawa adds 1-2 days to the processing time.
- An application lodged less than 15 calendar days before the intended departure may be accepted at VFS, but the final decision might be taken after the date of departure.
You can find the information on which third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation on the Visa Code Handbook, Annex 16 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%c3%a5ndbogen).
Please note that case processing may be extended if further scrutiny is required
Also missing documentation at the time of the appointment at VFS may delay the processing time.
Please also note that the Consulate General cannot be held responsible for technical difficulties, human errors or shipping delays in the processing of visa applications.
It is the applicant's responsibility to notify the Danish Consulate General immediately, if incorrect information on the visa sticker has been
                            detected.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
You should apply for business visa if you are travelling to Denmark for business purposes. 
A visa allows you to stay a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period in Denmark and is normally valid for the entire Schengen region. The Schengen countries are Austria, Belgium, the Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France,
                            Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland. 
Decisions about visa applications will normally be 15 days from the day the Danish Consulate General in New York receives the application, excluding submission and administration time at VFS. In some cases applications will require further enquiry or documentation implying that the processing time might exceed the 15 days.
If your company or organization is enrolled in the Red Carpet Program, you will be offered a visa fast track service only in Toronto, implying inter alia a shorter processing time. For more information about the Red Carpet Program,
                            please click here (#)
Applicants might also be asked to appear for an Interview via the Danish Embassy in Ottawa.
A visa does not allow you to work in Denmark unless the Immigration Service has explicitly granted you this right.
For more information about how to apply for a visa, please
                            click here  (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
You should apply for business visa if you are travelling to Denmark for business purposes. 
A visa allows you to stay a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period in Denmark and is normally valid for the entire Schengen region. The Schengen countries are Austria, Belgium, the Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France,
                            Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland. 
Decisions about visa applications will normally be 15 days from the day the Danish Consulate General in New York receives the application, excluding submission and administration time at VFS. In some cases applications will require further enquiry or documentation implying that the processing time might exceed the 15 days.
If your company or organization is enrolled in the Red Carpet Program, you will be offered a visa fast track service only in Toronto, implying inter alia a shorter processing time. For more information about the Red Carpet Program,
                            please click here (#)
Applicants might also be asked to appear for an Interview via the Danish Embassy in Ottawa.
A visa does not allow you to work in Denmark unless the Immigration Service has explicitly granted you this right.
For more information about how to apply for a visa, please
                            click here  (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa)

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Tourist]

[Table:   Tourist]
Please Note:
- Applicants applying from Edmonton, Toronto and Vancouver need to pay mandatory courier charges of Euro 6 (CAD 9.5) towards one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Ottawa back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across Canada
- The fee is non-refundable
- All VFS Fees can be paid by Debit/Credit Card only.
- AMEX is not accepted.

## Documents Required
Gather your documents 
Please gather the relevant documents provided in the document checklist based on the purpose of your trip 
Please note that your passport should be valid for a minimum of three months after expiry of the visa; must be issued within the last ten years and must have two blank pages - if you do not have two blank pages to which a visa
                            can be affixed, a new passport must be obtained before submitting your application.

## Photo Specifications
The photographs must meet the following ICAO requirements: 
- Colour photographs
- Light background
- Taken up close
- Well lit
- Taken straight on
- Up-to-date
- Without headgear
- Without sunglasses
- With mouth closed
- Photograph should measure 35 mm x 45 mm (1 3/8 x 1 ¾ inch)
- Height of head should measure 70 - 80 percent of height of photograph or 32 mm – 36 mm (1 2/8 to 1 ½ inch)

## Processing Time
STANDARD PROCESSING TIMES 
Due to unusually high volumes of visa applications the processing time is currently longer than usual, and there are fewer available appointment slots at VFS Global.
The processing time for visa applications are currently up to 15 days. We regret the inconvenience this may cause, but assure that applications are handled as swiftly as possible.
A visa application can be launched as early as 6 months prior to the arrival date in Schengen, and we strongly encourage applicants to apply as early as possible. It is possible to apply up to 6 months prior to the desired date of entry Here is why:
- It can take up to 14 days to obtain an appointment with VFS for submitting your application.
- Case processing does not begin until the application arrives at the Consulate General of Denmark in New York. Hence, your application is not launched when you apply online, or when you have your appointment with VFS. The shipping
                                of your documents to and from the Royal Danish Embassy in Ottawa adds 1-2 days to the processing time.
- An application lodged less than 15 calendar days before the intended departure may be accepted at VFS, but the final decision might be taken after the date of departure.
You can find the information on which third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation on the Visa Code Handbook, Annex 16 (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/da/Lovstof/Visumkodeks-mm_/Visumh%c3%a5ndbogen).
Please note that case processing may be extended if further scrutiny is required
Also missing documentation at the time of the appointment at VFS may delay the processing time.
Please also note that the Consulate General cannot be held responsible for technical difficulties, human errors or shipping delays in the processing of visa applications.
It is the applicant's responsibility to notify the Danish Consulate General immediately, if incorrect information on the visa sticker has been
                            detected.
                        

## Online Application Form
- Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa Portal (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form.
Note:
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS by clicking here. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted, and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment. For appointment related question, kindly reach out to our support desk (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/can/en/dnk/contact-us) or visa application centre.
Refer to sample application form - CLICK HERE

Overview
You should apply for a tourist visa, if you are travelling to Denmark for tourism purposes.
A visa allows you to stay a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period in Denmark and is normally valid for the entire Schengen region.
Decisions about visa applications will normally be 15 days from the day the Danish Consulate General in New York receives the application, excluding submission and administration time at VFS. In some cases applications will require further enquiry or documentation implying that the processing time might exceed the 15 days.
Applicants might also be asked to appear for an Interview via the Danish Embassy in Ottawa.
A visa does not allow you to work in Denmark unless the Immigration Service has explicitly granted you this right.
For more information about how to apply for a visa, please click here (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/)

## Overview
You should apply for a tourist visa, if you are travelling to Denmark for tourism purposes.
A visa allows you to stay a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period in Denmark and is normally valid for the entire Schengen region.
Decisions about visa applications will normally be 15 days from the day the Danish Consulate General in New York receives the application, excluding submission and administration time at VFS. In some cases applications will require further enquiry or documentation implying that the processing time might exceed the 15 days.
Applicants might also be asked to appear for an Interview via the Danish Embassy in Ottawa.
A visa does not allow you to work in Denmark unless the Immigration Service has explicitly granted you this right.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit
visa_types.documents_required:
Once you have created you Case Order ID and paid your application fee, you should gather the required documentation to either the Danish Immigration Service or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI). Make sure to hand in the case order ID along with the required documentation and your biometrics.
If you should only supply your biometrics as your application is already submitted to the relevant authority in Denmark, you must bring a copy of the confirmation letter (with your unique case order ID) stating that the application has been submitted online along with your passport.
Please note: Your original passport must be submitted to VFS Global and will be sent to the Royal Danish Embassy in Ottawa for verification purposes. The passport will be returned to you usually within a week. For more information please visit: https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit)
The Danish mission may ask for further documentation on behalf of the Danish Immigration Service or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) as well as request a personal interview at the Danish mission or at the Visa Application Centre after you have handed in your application, documentation, and biometrics.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to stay in Denmark longer than 90 days or want to work in Denmark, you must apply for a residence and work permit. You also need to apply for a work permit if you will be doing unpaid work or seek sideline employment.
For information of residence and work permits including application form, please visit www.newtodenmark.dk (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to stay in Denmark longer than 90 days or want to work in Denmark, you must apply for a residence and work permit. You also need to apply for a work permit if you will be doing unpaid work or seek sideline employment.
For information of residence and work permits including application form, please visit www.newtodenmark.dk (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB).

## Fee
When applying for a Residence Permit to Denmark from abroad, the following fees will be payable:
- In most cases, a fee will be payable to Danish Immigration Service (DIS) or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) in Copenhagen for processing your application. The fee amount will vary depending on the type of residence permit you are applying for. This fee is payable by credit card directly to DIS/SIRI through the payment portal of their website (link below). It is important that you pay this fee prior to your appointment at VFS Global. Remember to bring your receipt. For information regarding the applicable fee for your type of residence permit, please visit the DIS/SIRI website and select the category for which you are applying. (Notice applications for a residence permit under the Working Holiday category are exempt from this fee)

https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply)
- When submitting an application for a residence permit to Denmark from abroad, there will be an additional fee payable to the Danish Ministry of Foreign Affairs. This fee, is payable directly to the Danish Ministry of Foreign Affairs online by credit card via the below link, and should be paid prior to your appointment with VFS Global. Be sure to bring the receipt, which contains an important reference number.
 
https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
- VFS Global will charge a service fee (Euro 27) for receiving your application and obtaining your biometric features. In addition, you could be charged an optional courier fee to have your passport returned directly back to your residence, (no courier fees applicable, if picking up your passport at VFS Global). Other optional fees may apply for photocopy- and printing services, if applicable, as well as optional SMS tracking fees.
Fees payable to VFS Global are paid locally, when you go for your appointment. Acceptable payment methods include VISA, MasterCard and debit. For more information about the VFS service fees, please visit our website: http://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/consular-fees/ (http://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/consular-fees/) (click VFS Global fees)

## Documentation and biometrics
Once you have created you Case Order ID and paid your application fee, you should gather the required documentation to either the Danish Immigration Service or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI). Make sure to hand in the case order ID along with the required documentation and your biometrics.
If you should only supply your biometrics as your application is already submitted to the relevant authority in Denmark, you must bring a copy of the confirmation letter (with your unique case order ID) stating that the application has been submitted online along with your passport.
Please note: Your original passport must be submitted to VFS Global and will be sent to the Royal Danish Embassy in Ottawa for verification purposes. The passport will be returned to you usually within a week. For more information please visit: https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit (https://canada.um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/residence-permit)
The Danish mission may ask for further documentation on behalf of the Danish Immigration Service or the Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI) as well as request a personal interview at the Danish mission or at the Visa Application Centre after you have handed in your application, documentation, and biometrics.
